title: Celtics' Jayson Tatum listed as questionable for G7 vs. 76ers
content: Celtics superstar Jayson Tatum was added to the injury report with left knee soreness Saturday afternoon, roughly six hours before the playoff Game 7 against Philadelphia.
corpus:
Boston Celtics superstar Jayson Tatum was added to the injury report Saturday afternoon with left knee soreness, putting his availability in doubt for Game 7 against the Philadelphia 76ers in their Eastern Conference first-round playoff series.

Tatum departed Thursday's Game 6 loss with 4:03 remaining in the third quarter and did not return. Coach Joe Mazzulla initially downplayed any injury concern, stating the player simply needed stretching and treatment. However, Tatum appeared on the official injury report the following day, listed as questionable.

The timing of this development is significant given that Tatum suffered a torn Achilles in his right leg last May. He returned in March and had been performing at an elite level before Thursday's game.

Regarding his status before the game, Mazzulla said: "He just went to the back and just got stretched, got some treatment. That was about it." Tatum added that his leg felt stiff but noted the team's decision to remove starters with the game out of reach.

This Game 7 represents the 23rd playoff meeting between these franchises—the most in NBA history between any two teams. Boston holds a commanding historical advantage, winning six consecutive playoff matchups dating back to 1982. The Celtics also lead the series 3-1 and have a perfect 32-0 record when holding such leads in best-of-seven series.

title: NBA championship and Finals MVP odds: Knicks move to 19-1 to win it all; Lakers advance
content: The Knicks improved their championship odds to 19-1 following their playoff series-clinching victory, while other teams' odds shifted based on recent performances.
corpus:
The article reports on shifting championship odds following the first round of the 2026 NBA playoffs. The New York Knicks advanced past Atlanta and remain at 18-1 to win the title, while the Los Angeles Lakers moved on by eliminating Houston at 28-1 odds.

A major surprise came with Denver's elimination. The Nuggets, who entered the postseason with the fourth-best odds at +850, fell to Minnesota in the first round. The Timberwolves now face San Antonio in the conference semifinals but carry significant injury concerns affecting Anthony Edwards, Donte DiVincenzo, and Ayo Dosunmu, pushing their championship odds to 180-1.

Boston's championship prospects weakened slightly following their struggles against Philadelphia. The Celtics entered at +550 but are now +650 after dropping games to the 76ers. Boston faces a Game 7 showdown as 8.5-point favorites. Philadelphia's odds improved from 250-1 to 120-1 following their surprising playoff performance.

In the Finals MVP race, Oklahoma City's Shai Gilgeous-Alexander leads at -110 odds, followed by San Antonio's Victor Wembanyama at +425. Boston's Jaylen Brown and Jayson Tatum share 12-1 odds.

title: LeBron jokes about Texans' Will Anderson Jr., Bronny resemblance
content: LeBron James made lighthearted comments about social media comparisons between Houston Texans player Will Anderson Jr. and his son Bronny James.
corpus:
During the Lakers' playoff victory over the Rockets on Friday, LeBron James humorously addressed the viral online comparisons between Houston Texans defensive end Will Anderson Jr. and his son Bronny.

After crashing to the floor in the third quarter, James received help from Anderson, who was sitting courtside. Following the game, James joked about their supposed connection, calling Anderson "my fourth child" and playfully suggesting that Anderson and Bronny are actually twins.

"It was good to see him," James said of the encounter. He humorously stated: "Him and Bronny are twins and don't nobody know it. Me and Savannah have been trying to keep it under wraps" — before adding a disclaimer that he meant no disrespect to Anderson's actual parents.

The resemblance between the two has sparked considerable social media buzz over the years, with users frequently comparing their appearances side-by-side. The Houston Texans organization embraced the humor by reposting James's comments along with a Spider-Man meme showing two identical figures pointing at each other.

Anderson responded positively on Instagram, sharing video of the moment and calling James "a freaking legend." Meanwhile, James praised Anderson as an exceptional football player.

In the game itself, James delivered 28 points, eight assists, and seven rebounds as the Lakers defeated Houston 98-78 to advance from the first round.

title: Betting tips, DFS picks for 76ers-Celtics: All of the factors in Game 7
content: Expert analysis examines Saturday's Game 7 matchup and provides betting strategies and daily fantasy basketball recommendations for the contest.
corpus:
This matchup marks the ninth Game 7 between Philadelphia and Boston, the most playoff meetings between any two NBA teams. The Celtics enter as significant favorites after dominating twice by 30+ points, while the 76ers have won all three home games by keeping Boston under 100 points.

Boston's Strategy: The Celtics aim to control the possession battle through offensive rebounding and ball security. Their 3-point shooting efficiency and volume will be critical, though Philadelphia's perimeter defense has forced them to shoot poorly from distance in recent contests.

Philadelphia's Path: Defense is paramount for the 76ers. They'll deploy Paul George, VJ Edgecombe, and Kelly Oubre Jr. to contain Jayson Tatum and Jaylen Brown. Joel Embiid has been effective as a passing hub when defenses double him.

Betting Recommendations:
- Paul George Over 15.5 Points (-124): George has scored at least 16 in every series game. He'll continue hunting mismatches and receiving consistent touches.
- Jayson Tatum Over 5.5 Assists (-131): Expect increased playmaking as the Sixers' wing defenders pressure him around screens.

Fantasy Values:
- Jayson Tatum ($16,500): Will likely lead in touches and shot attempts
- Tyrese Maxey ($15,000): Exploits Boston's drop coverage with layups and floaters
- VJ Edgecombe ($12,300): Rookie ready for the moment with all-around impact

Game Details: 7:30 p.m. ET, NBC | Celtics -8.5 | Over/Under 205.5

title: NBA playoffs 2026: Three Game 7s! What will decide each showdown
content: Three Eastern Conference first-round series reach Game 7 decisively. Analysis covers the Celtics-76ers, Magic-Pistons, and Cavaliers-Raptors matchups.
corpus:
The 2026 NBA first round concludes with three Game 7s—all in the Eastern Conference—marking only the second time since 2003 that the opening round has produced this many decisive matchups.

76ers vs. Celtics (Saturday, 7:30 p.m. ET)

For Philadelphia to advance: The 76ers must maintain the formula that worked recently. Joel Embiid continues manipulating Boston's defense through double-team kicks or isolation scoring, while Tyrese Maxey exploits the lack of defensive answers. Improved three-point shooting would be transformative, as Philadelphia has won without needing to outscore Boston.

For Boston to win: Three-point accuracy is essential. The Celtics are 17-1 in the playoffs under Joe Mazzulla when shooting 40% or better from beyond the arc, versus 19-19 otherwise. Controlling offensive rebounds and the possession game remains critical.

Key factor: Neemias Queta's performance. Boston's center has fouled excessively against Joel Embiid (24 fouls in six games) and must stay on the floor while providing defensive resistance.

Pistons vs. Magic (Sunday, 3:30 p.m. ET)

For Orlando to win: Early physicality and defensive intensity, mirroring Game 1 strategy. However, Franz Wagner's injury absence complicates this approach, requiring role players to elevate their contributions.

For Detroit to win: The Pistons must dominate the paint through Jalen Duren's physical presence while maintaining the suffocating defense that fueled their remarkable 24-point comeback in Game 6.

Key factor: Three-point shooting. Detroit ranks last in the playoffs in three-point makes and percentage—a historically poor stretch that cannot persist if the team hopes to advance.

Cavaliers vs. Raptors (Sunday, 7:30 p.m. ET)

For Toronto to win: Convert Game 7 into a defensive battle. The Raptors have gained confidence through grit-based basketball and transition defense, creating pressure on Cleveland's home court.

For Cleveland to win: Limit transition opportunities by minimizing turnovers. The Cavaliers held just one turnover during their Game 6 fourth-quarter rally.

Key factor: James Harden's performance. In seven career Game 7s, Harden has averaged just 19.3 points on 35% shooting, including 22% from three. However, he has scored over 20 points in every win against Toronto this series.

title: Celtics, 76ers set for winner-take-all game 7
content: The Boston Celtics (56-26) host the Philadelphia 76ers (45-37) in an Eastern Conference quarterfinal Game 7 playoff matchup.
corpus:
The Philadelphia 76ers face the Boston Celtics in a winner-take-all Eastern Conference first-round matchup on May 2, 2026, at TD Garden. The series is tied 3-3.

Team Records:
- Celtics: 56-26 (2nd in East)
- 76ers: 45-37 (7th in East)

Betting Line: Celtics -7.5; over/under 205.5

Key Storylines: In their most recent meeting, the 76ers defeated the Celtics 106-93. Tyrese Maxey led Philadelphia with 30 points, while Jaylen Brown paced Boston with 18 points.

The Celtics boast the league's top defense, allowing just 107.2 points per game while holding opponents to 44.2% shooting. Boston's 36-16 conference record demonstrates their dominance within the East.

Top Performers: Brown averages 28.7 points, 6.9 rebounds, and 5.1 assists for the Celtics. Jayson Tatum has contributed 18.7 points over the last ten games. Maxey leads the 76ers' recent production, averaging 25.0 points and 3.9 rebounds across their last ten contests. Quentin Grimes adds 13.4 points per game.

Recent Form: The Celtics have gone 6-4 over their last ten games, averaging 112.2 points while shooting 45.5% from the field. The 76ers stand at 5-5, averaging 105.2 points per game.

Injury Report: Joel Embiid remains day-to-day with an abdominal injury for Philadelphia. Boston lists no injuries.

The game airs on NBC/Peacock at 7:30 p.m. EDT.

title: Thunder's Shai Gilgeous-Alexander wins regular-season MVP award
content: Oklahoma City's Shai Gilgeous-Alexander claimed his first Most Valuable Player award after leading the Thunder to the league's best record at 64-18.
corpus:
Oklahoma City Thunder guard Shai Gilgeous-Alexander was named the 2025-26 NBA Most Valuable Player on Monday, becoming the first Thunder player to win the award since Kevin Durant in 2014.

Gilgeous-Alexander averaged 32.4 points, 6.1 assists, and 5.3 rebounds per game while shooting 53.1% from the field and 38.7% from three-point range. He led Oklahoma City to a franchise-best 64-18 record and the No. 1 seed in the Western Conference playoffs.

"This award belongs to my teammates," Gilgeous-Alexander said during the ceremony. "They trusted me every night to make the right play, and they made my job easier. I don't take that for granted."

Gilgeous-Alexander received 87 of 100 first-place votes, comfortably outdistancing San Antonio's Victor Wembanyama, who finished second, and Cleveland's Donovan Mitchell, who placed third. He becomes only the fourth guard in the past decade to win the award.

Thunder coach Mark Daigneault called Gilgeous-Alexander "the most complete player in the league right now" and praised his ability to elevate his game in critical moments. Oklahoma City won 14 of its final 18 games largely on the strength of his performances.

title: Wembanyama puts up 38-point triple-double in Spurs' Game 5 win over Timberwolves
content: Victor Wembanyama delivered a historic 38-point, 14-rebound, 10-block performance to push San Antonio past Minnesota and take a 3-2 series lead.
corpus:
Victor Wembanyama produced one of the most dominant individual playoff performances in recent memory Tuesday night, finishing with 38 points, 14 rebounds, 10 blocks, and six assists as the San Antonio Spurs defeated the Minnesota Timberwolves 114-97 to take a 3-2 series lead in their Western Conference first-round matchup.

Wembanyama became just the second player in NBA playoff history to record at least 35 points, 10 rebounds, and 10 blocks in a single game, joining Kareem Abdul-Jabbar from 1974. He was an imposing presence on both ends of the floor, altering or rejecting virtually every Minnesota drive attempt.

"He was playing chess out there tonight and we were playing checkers," Timberwolves coach Chris Finch admitted after the loss. "We had no answers. When someone like that is in complete command of a game, there's not much you can do."

Minnesota's Anthony Edwards, dealing with a sprained right ankle that limited him to 28 minutes, managed 21 points but shot just 7-of-22 from the field. The Timberwolves now face elimination in Game 6 at home.

Wembanyama, asked after the game whether he felt this was his best professional performance, smiled before saying: "There will be better ones." His confidence appears fully justified through two playoff rounds.

title: Knicks eliminate Hawks in five games, advance to second round
content: New York held Atlanta to 89 points in a clinching Game 5 victory at Madison Square Garden, advancing to the Eastern Conference semifinals for the second straight season.
corpus:
The New York Knicks eliminated the Atlanta Hawks from the 2026 NBA playoffs with a 108-89 victory in Game 5 on Sunday at Madison Square Garden, setting up a second-round matchup against the winner of the Celtics-76ers series.

Jalen Brunson led the Knicks with 29 points and eight assists, while Karl-Anthony Towns added 22 points and 11 rebounds. New York held Atlanta below 95 points in four of the five games, demonstrating the defensive improvement under coach Tom Thibodeau's adjustments.

Trae Young, who had carried the Hawks with an average of 28.4 points in the series, was held to 17 on 6-of-19 shooting. The Knicks deployed Mikal Bridges as Young's primary defender in the fourth quarter, limiting the All-Star guard to no field goals over the final 12 minutes.

"We did what we set out to do — defend at the highest level and make them uncomfortable," Brunson said. "Our guys bought in from the start and that's why we're moving on."

The Knicks' victory gave them back-to-back second-round appearances for the first time since 1999-2000. Their championship odds at sportsbooks improved from 22-1 to 18-1 following the clinching win.

title: Anthony Davis day-to-day with left knee contusion after Lakers' series win
content: Los Angeles Lakers center Anthony Davis sustained a left knee contusion in the fourth quarter of the series-clinching Game 5 win over Houston and is considered day-to-day.
corpus:
Los Angeles Lakers center Anthony Davis is listed as day-to-day after sustaining a left knee contusion in the fourth quarter of Saturday's 101-88 series-clinching win over the Houston Rockets, the team announced Sunday morning.

Davis took a hard fall after a collision with Alperen Sengun with 6:32 remaining in the fourth quarter. He briefly left the game but returned to the floor two minutes later and finished with 24 points and 13 rebounds. Despite the apparent discomfort, Davis played through the remainder of the contest.

Lakers coach JJ Redick said the team would monitor Davis closely over the next 48 hours. "He's tough. He wanted to come back out there. We'll get more imaging done and reassess before we talk about the second round," Redick said.

LeBron James, who logged 36 minutes in the win, also received treatment on his right ankle after the game but is not expected to miss any time. The Lakers have roughly six days before their second-round series begins, pending the outcome of other first-round matchups.

Davis averaged 26.8 points, 11.4 rebounds, and 3.1 blocks during the first-round series against Houston — a performance that drew widespread praise and reinforced his status as one of the premier two-way players in the league.

title: Donovan Mitchell drops 45 in Cavaliers' Game 6 comeback win over Raptors
content: Donovan Mitchell scored 45 points, including 17 in the fourth quarter, to power Cleveland's 118-109 comeback win over Toronto and force a decisive Game 7.
corpus:
Donovan Mitchell refused to let the Cleveland Cavaliers' season end Thursday night, delivering a performance for the ages to force a Game 7 against the Toronto Raptors. Mitchell finished with 45 points — matching his career playoff high — on 16-of-28 shooting, including 5-of-10 from three, as Cleveland rallied from a 14-point third-quarter deficit to win 118-109.

Mitchell's 17 fourth-quarter points were the story of the night. He attacked the Toronto defense relentlessly in the final frame, converting a string of mid-range jumpers and drawing fouls at a high rate. He finished 8-of-9 from the free-throw line in the quarter alone.

"He's one of the best playoff performers in the game," Cavaliers coach Kenny Atkinson said afterward. "You don't blink in a moment like that — you give him the ball and you trust him. That's what we did."

Jamahl Mosley's Raptors led 89-75 entering the fourth quarter on the back of 29 points from Scottie Barnes. But Barnes faded down the stretch, scoring just two points after the 8-minute mark as Cleveland's defensive pressure mounted.

Game 7 will be played Sunday at Rocket Mortgage FieldHouse in Cleveland, where the Cavaliers are 32-9 this season including playoffs. Mitchell has scored at least 35 points in three of six games in this series.

title: OKC Thunder roll past Dallas in four games, set franchise record for playoff wins
content: The Oklahoma City Thunder swept the Dallas Mavericks in the first round, setting a franchise record with their 12th consecutive playoff win dating back to last season.
corpus:
The Oklahoma City Thunder completed a first-round sweep of the Dallas Mavericks with a 124-108 victory Sunday at Paycom Center, extending the franchise's playoff winning streak to a record 12 consecutive games.

Shai Gilgeous-Alexander led Oklahoma City with 31 points and nine assists, while Jalen Williams added 24 points and Chet Holmgren contributed 18 points and nine rebounds. The Thunder led by as many as 23 points in the third quarter and never allowed Dallas to pull within single digits after halftime.

Dallas, without an injured Kyrie Irving for the final three games, struggled to find consistent offensive production. Luka Doncic averaged 28.2 points in the series but received little support, and the Mavericks' defense — ranked 24th in the regular season — had no answer for OKC's relentless ball movement.

The Thunder outscored Dallas by an average of 16.8 points per game in the series — the largest margin of victory in a four-game sweep since the 2017 Golden State Warriors. They advance to the Western Conference semifinals, where they will face the winner of San Antonio-Minnesota.

"We don't celebrate sweeps. We have bigger goals," Gilgeous-Alexander said. "This team is built for a long run. We're just getting started."

title: Steph Curry to miss remainder of playoffs with torn left labrum
content: Golden State Warriors guard Stephen Curry will undergo surgery on a torn left shoulder labrum and will not return this postseason, the team announced Friday.
corpus:
The Golden State Warriors announced Friday that Stephen Curry will miss the remainder of the 2026 playoffs after sustaining a torn left shoulder labrum in Wednesday's Game 3 loss to the Sacramento Kings. Curry will undergo surgery next week.

Curry was injured when he dove for a loose ball in the second quarter and landed hard on his left shoulder. He grimaced immediately and was attended to by team trainers before eventually returning to the bench. He did not return to the game and underwent imaging Thursday that confirmed the diagnosis.

"This is devastating news for Steph and for our organization," Warriors coach Steve Kerr said. "His health is the priority. We'll rally around our guys and compete, but there's no replacing what he means to us."

With Curry out, Golden State — already trailing 2-1 in the series — faces a steep climb. The Warriors are 11-44 in playoff games without Curry since 2019. Sacramento, seeded sixth in the West, has outplayed Golden State in every game of the series, holding the Warriors below 104 points twice.

Curry, 37, had been averaging 24.3 points through the first three games. His recovery timeline is estimated at five to six months, putting him on track to be fully healthy for training camp in October.

title: Giannis Antetokounmpo posts 41-point double-double in Bucks' Game 4 win
content: Giannis Antetokounmpo scored 41 points and grabbed 17 rebounds as Milwaukee evened its first-round series against Indiana at two games apiece.
corpus:
Giannis Antetokounmpo was unstoppable Sunday, posting 41 points and 17 rebounds on 16-of-22 shooting as the Milwaukee Bucks defeated the Indiana Pacers 117-103 to level their Eastern Conference first-round series at 2-2.

The two-time MVP attacked the basket relentlessly, drawing 14 free-throw attempts and converting 11. Indiana's Myles Turner, the primary defender tasked with slowing Giannis, fouled out with 4:41 remaining in the fourth quarter after committing two quick fouls in 47 seconds.

"You can scheme against him all you want, but when he gets into that zone there's no X-and-O solution," Pacers coach Rick Carlisle said. "He's just too powerful and too skilled."

Typrese Haliburton had 26 points and 11 assists for Indiana but turned the ball over six times — including two costly possessions in the final two minutes when the Pacers trailed by only seven. Milwaukee turned those miscues into seven consecutive points to seal the win.

Brooks Lopez added 18 points and five blocks for Milwaukee. The Bucks have now won Game 4 in nine consecutive playoff series dating back to 2019. Game 5 is scheduled for Tuesday in Indianapolis.

title: NBA Draft lottery results: Washington Wizards earn No. 1 pick
content: The Washington Wizards won the 2026 NBA Draft lottery, securing the first overall pick in June's draft for the first time in franchise history.
corpus:
The Washington Wizards secured the No. 1 overall pick in the 2026 NBA Draft on Tuesday night, defying their 14% lottery odds in a result that sent the organization into celebration and sent rival executives back to the drawing board.

Washington, which finished the season 17-65, had the third-best odds in the lottery behind San Antonio and Utah. Commissioner Adam Silver revealed the selection order in reverse, building toward the announcement that Washington had leapfrogged every team ahead of it.

"This is a franchise-changing moment," Wizards general manager Will Davis said. "It's rare in this league to be in position to select a generational talent, and that's exactly what we intend to do."

The pick is widely expected to be used on Cooper Flagg, the Duke forward who finished his freshman season averaging 21.8 points, 10.1 rebounds, and 4.4 assists. Flagg is considered the consensus No. 1 prospect since LeBron James entered the draft in 2003.

The Charlotte Hornets fell to the No. 2 spot despite having the best odds, while Utah landed at No. 3. The Miami Heat, entering the lottery for the first time in four years, slid to No. 7.

title: Nikola Jokic wins fourth MVP despite Nuggets' first-round exit
content: Nikola Jokic claimed his fourth Most Valuable Player award Wednesday, becoming the first player to win the honor in a season his team failed to advance past the first round of the playoffs.
corpus:
Nikola Jokic became the most decorated MVP in modern NBA history Wednesday when the league announced he had won his fourth award, edging Oklahoma City's Shai Gilgeous-Alexander despite Denver's shocking first-round elimination by the Minnesota Timberwolves.

Jokic averaged 29.4 points, 13.8 rebounds, and 9.7 assists per game — the closest any player has come to averaging a 30-14-10 season since the stat was officially tracked. He received 61 of 100 first-place votes, compared to 35 for Gilgeous-Alexander.

The decision drew debate given Denver's first-round exit. Critics argued that MVP voters should factor in team success; supporters countered that no individual player in the league's history has elevated a team's performance as dramatically. Denver went 29-53 without Jokic last season, compared to 54-28 with him.

"I don't care about the record or the history," Jokic said through an interpreter. "The award is nice but losing is not nice. I want to win a championship."

Denver coach Michael Malone expressed disappointment over the playoff result but unequivocal pride in his franchise player. Jokic becomes the first center to win four MVPs, surpassing Kareem Abdul-Jabbar and Moses Malone's three apiece.

title: Trae Young requests trade from Hawks following first-round exit
content: Atlanta Hawks guard Trae Young has formally requested a trade, sources told ESPN, citing a desire to compete for a championship after six seasons with the organization.
corpus:
Atlanta Hawks star Trae Young has formally requested a trade, multiple sources told ESPN on Monday, accelerating an offseason that figures to reshape the Eastern Conference landscape before next season.

Young, 27, informed Hawks ownership of his desire to leave following Atlanta's five-game first-round elimination by the Knicks. He has three years remaining on his maximum contract at roughly $47 million per season, which will complicate any potential deal.

The move ends the Young era in Atlanta, where he has spent his entire career since being drafted fifth overall in 2018. He helped rebuild the franchise and led the Hawks to the Eastern Conference Finals in 2021, but the organization has stalled since, failing to advance past the first round in each of the past three seasons.

Teams with the cap space and assets to acquire Young include Miami, Golden State, and Portland, sources said. The Knicks, Celtics, and Lakers have all been mentioned in preliminary conversations as potential suitors, though each faces significant cap constraints.

Young averaged 26.9 points and 10.8 assists this season — numbers that place him among the elite playmakers in the league — and is expected to attract significant interest despite the substantial contract obligation.

title: Cavaliers outlast Raptors in overtime thriller to close out series in seven
content: Cleveland advanced to the second round after Donovan Mitchell hit the go-ahead shot with 1.2 seconds remaining in overtime to seal a 109-107 Game 7 win over Toronto.
corpus:
Donovan Mitchell delivered the defining shot of the 2026 first round Sunday night, driving left and floating a one-handed layup over Scottie Barnes' outstretched arm with 1.2 seconds remaining in overtime to give the Cleveland Cavaliers a 109-107 Game 7 victory over the Toronto Raptors at Rocket Mortgage FieldHouse.

Mitchell finished with 38 points, eight rebounds, and five assists in 46 grueling minutes. His layup, scored while being fouled — though the official did not make the call — proved to be the decisive basket. Toronto had one final possession but RJ Barrett's half-court heave was off target as the buzzer sounded.

"My teammates got me that shot. I just had to trust my footwork and let it go," Mitchell said. "This city has been behind us all year. That's who I was thinking about when it left my hand."

The Raptors, who trailed 3-1 in the series before winning Games 5 and 6, pushed Cleveland to the absolute limit. Scottie Barnes matched Mitchell nearly bucket for bucket, finishing with 33 points and 12 rebounds. James Harden added 24 points and 11 assists.

Cleveland advances to face the winner of the Celtics-76ers series. The Cavaliers won their last championship in 2016 and have not reached the conference finals since 2018.

title: Pistons stun Magic in Game 7, reach second round for first time since 2008
content: Detroit's Jalen Duren posted a 22-point, 19-rebound double-double to lead the Pistons past Orlando 97-88 in Game 7, ending the organization's 18-year second-round drought.
corpus:
The Detroit Pistons completed one of the most improbable playoff runs in recent memory Sunday, defeating the Orlando Magic 97-88 in Game 7 at Little Caesars Arena to advance to the Eastern Conference semifinals for the first time since 2008.

Jalen Duren was the engine that drove Detroit's victory, posting 22 points, 19 rebounds, and four blocks. The 23-year-old center was a force on both ends throughout, converting 9-of-12 field-goal attempts while patrolling the paint with relentless energy. It was the finest game of his career at the most critical moment.

"Eighteen years is a long time," Pistons coach Monty Williams said, his voice cracking. "This city deserves this. These players put the work in, nobody respected them, and they went out and earned everything they got."

Orlando, playing without Franz Wagner for the fifth straight game due to a hamstring injury, could not generate consistent half-court offense. Paolo Banchero finished with 28 points and 10 rebounds but received minimal support — no other Magic starter reached 14 points.

Detroit will face the New York Knicks in the second round. The Pistons finished the regular season 38-44 and were widely predicted to lose in the first round, making their advancement the largest upset of the 2026 postseason.

title: NBA trade deadline: Lakers acquire Damian Lillard from Bucks in blockbuster deal
content: The Los Angeles Lakers acquired Damian Lillard from Milwaukee in a three-team trade at Thursday's deadline, pairing the veteran guard alongside LeBron James and Anthony Davis.
corpus:
The Los Angeles Lakers completed a blockbuster trade Thursday at the NBA trade deadline, acquiring Milwaukee Bucks guard Damian Lillard in a three-team deal that also involved the Portland Trail Blazers, according to multiple league sources.

Lillard, 35, comes to Los Angeles in exchange for D'Angelo Russell, Rui Hachimura, a 2026 first-round pick, and two second-round selections. Portland received salary-cap relief through expiring contracts from Milwaukee, completing the three-team framework.

The move unites Lillard with LeBron James and Anthony Davis, forming one of the most experienced frontcourt-and-backcourt pairings in recent league memory. The Lakers enter the trade as 6th seeds in the Western Conference but believe the addition of Lillard — a seven-time All-Star — gives them true championship viability.

"Dame fits what we're trying to do," Lakers general manager Rob Pelinka said. "He's an elite shot creator, he takes pressure off LeBron and AD, and he knows how to win. That's the profile we wanted."

Lillard, who was averaging 22.7 points and 7.4 assists for Milwaukee before the trade, had made his desire to compete for a championship known to Bucks management. Milwaukee will receive draft compensation and several young assets in return.

title: Spurs advance past Timberwolves in six games, Wembanyama averages 34 in series
content: San Antonio eliminated Minnesota to reach the Western Conference semifinals for the first time since 2019, powered by Victor Wembanyama's dominant six-game series average of 34.2 points.
corpus:
The San Antonio Spurs eliminated the Minnesota Timberwolves in six games Thursday night, defeating them 118-104 to set up a highly anticipated Western Conference semifinal against the Oklahoma City Thunder. Victor Wembanyama averaged 34.2 points, 12.6 rebounds, and 6.3 blocks in the series — the most statistically dominant six-game playoff run by any player since Shaquille O'Neal in the 2000 Finals.

Wembanyama had 29 points and eight blocks in the clinching game, neutralizing Anthony Edwards' attempts to drive and score at the rim. Minnesota's Anthony Edwards, hampered by an ankle injury, managed 24 points but shot just 39% from the field across the six-game set.

"We knew what this matchup meant," Wembanyama said through a measured smile after the win. "We put in the work since training camp. I'm proud of how we defended together — it was never just me."

Spurs coach Gregg Popovich, in his final season before planned retirement, embraced his young star on the court as the clock expired. San Antonio's supporting cast — led by Jeremy Sochan's 18 points in the clinching game — proved decisive in the series' latter stages.

The Spurs-Thunder second-round matchup pits the league's two most exciting young stars against each other. Wembanyama versus Gilgeous-Alexander is already being hailed as the defining rivalry of this generation.

title: Jaylen Brown agrees to super-max extension with Celtics
content: Jaylen Brown signed a five-year, $298 million super-max contract extension with the Boston Celtics, committing his prime years to the franchise through the 2030-31 season.
corpus:
The Boston Celtics and Jaylen Brown reached agreement Monday on a five-year, $298 million super-max extension, the largest contract in NBA history, ensuring that the 2024 Finals MVP will remain in Boston for the foreseeable future.

Brown, 29, was eligible for the designated rookie extension after Boston's championship run and had drawn interest from several large-market teams. His decision to stay removes the most significant uncertainty surrounding the defending Eastern Conference's best roster.

"I'm a Celtic. This is where I want to be," Brown said at a press conference. "We have unfinished business and I believe this group can compete for multiple championships. I wasn't going anywhere."

Celtics president of basketball operations Brad Stevens called the extension "the best investment this organization has made" and said the front office never seriously entertained scenarios that would have allowed Brown to reach free agency.

Brown averaged 28.7 points, 6.9 rebounds, and 5.1 assists this season while shooting 47.3% from the field and 38.4% from three. His two-way impact and ability to function as a primary ball-handler in the playoffs make him one of the most complete wings in the game.

title: Tyrese Haliburton named Most Improved Player of the Year
content: Indiana Pacers guard Tyrese Haliburton won the NBA's Most Improved Player award after averaging a career-best 29.1 points and 11.3 assists in his sixth professional season.
corpus:
Indiana Pacers guard Tyrese Haliburton was named the 2025-26 NBA Most Improved Player on Wednesday, acknowledging the remarkable offensive leap that turned him from an established All-Star into a legitimate MVP contender.

Haliburton averaged 29.1 points and 11.3 assists — both career highs — while shooting 49.2% from the field and 41.8% from three-point range. He posted 30-or-more points on 28 occasions and recorded a double-double in points and assists in 41 games, a single-season record.

"I worked on my finishing all summer," Haliburton said. "Last year people could take away my pull-up and I had nowhere to go. Now I can get to the rim and score in traffic. That changed everything."

Haliburton received 72 of 100 first-place votes, edging Memphis' Ja Morant and Denver's Michael Porter Jr. Indiana finished the regular season 51-31, a 12-game improvement from the prior year, with Haliburton's elevated play the primary driver of the turnaround.

Haliburton, 26, has now made three consecutive All-Star teams. Indiana coach Rick Carlisle called his development "one of the most complete offensive evolutions I've seen in 25 years of coaching."

title: Lakers-Nuggets second-round preview: Can LeBron and Davis slow Jokic?
content: The Los Angeles Lakers and Denver Nuggets open their Western Conference semifinal series Saturday, setting up a Jokic-versus-Davis battle that could define the series.
corpus:
The Los Angeles Lakers and Denver Nuggets open their Western Conference semifinal series Saturday at Ball Arena, renewing one of the decade's best playoff rivalries with a cast of familiar stars and significantly altered rosters.

The central matchup everyone is watching: Nikola Jokic versus Anthony Davis. In three prior playoff meetings, Jokic has averaged 29.1 points and 12.3 rebounds, while Davis has countered with 25.8 points and 11.7 boards. Neither player has truly dominated the other in any singular series.

The wrinkle this time is Damian Lillard. Acquired at the trade deadline, Lillard gives Los Angeles a second playmaker capable of operating in pick-and-roll — a weapon the Lakers lacked in previous Nuggets matchups. Denver's drop coverage invites exactly the sort of pull-up jumper at which Lillard excels.

Denver's concern is guard-level defense. The Nuggets ranked 22nd in defending the three-point line this season, and Lillard launches 12 attempts per game from beyond the arc. If he finds his rhythm early, Los Angeles could create game-flow problems that Jokic alone cannot compensate for.

Betting Line: Denver -2.5 | Over/Under 219.5

Key Matchup: Jamal Murray versus Lillard. The last time these two shared a playoff stage was the 2021 bubble — a series Murray won convincingly. The rematch carries significant personal stakes for both players.

title: Zion Williamson out indefinitely with recurring hamstring issue
content: New Orleans Pelicans forward Zion Williamson will miss an indefinite period after re-aggravating his right hamstring, the team announced Tuesday, casting doubt on his availability for the remainder of the regular season.
corpus:
The New Orleans Pelicans announced Tuesday that forward Zion Williamson will be sidelined indefinitely after re-aggravating his right hamstring during Monday's loss to the Memphis Grizzlies. The team said imaging has confirmed a Grade 2 strain and that no timeline for return will be established at this stage.

Williamson, 25, was injured in the second quarter while driving toward the basket and immediately grabbed the back of his right leg. He was helped off the court and did not return. It is the sixth significant soft-tissue injury of his professional career across seven seasons.

Coach Willie Green said the team would take a cautious approach. "Zion's health is always the priority. He's been playing some of the best basketball of his career and we want to make sure he comes back right — not just fast."

In 41 games this season before the injury, Williamson averaged 27.4 points, 7.8 rebounds, and 4.1 assists per game on 59.2% shooting. New Orleans entered Tuesday at 39-30, 6th in the Western Conference, making his availability for the playoff push critical.

The Pelicans have gone 12-18 in games Williamson missed over the past three seasons, a record that underscores how much the team's identity and performance are tied to his presence.

title: Thunder vs. Spurs second-round preview: The rivalry the league has been waiting for
content: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander and Victor Wembanyama meet in the playoffs for the first time as the Oklahoma City Thunder and San Antonio Spurs open their Western Conference semifinal series.
corpus:
The Western Conference semifinals deliver the matchup analysts and fans have been anticipating since Wembanyama entered the league — Oklahoma City's Shai Gilgeous-Alexander against San Antonio's Victor Wembanyama, two franchise cornerstones in their absolute prime colliding in the playoffs for the first time.

Gilgeous-Alexander, 27, comes in averaging 31.8 points and 6.4 assists in the postseason. His ability to score from every area of the floor — including an extraordinary 46% conversion rate on pull-up threes — makes him virtually impossible to game-plan against on a possession-by-possession basis.

Wembanyama, 22, answers with a profile that has no historical parallel. His wingspan and standing reach allow him to contest shots in ways no defender has ever achieved. Against Minnesota, he held opposing players to 38.1% shooting at the rim — a figure that doesn't account for all the drives that were simply abandoned before reaching their destination.

The series will hinge on Oklahoma City's ability to generate inside scoring despite Wembanyama's presence. The Thunder ranked third in paint points during the regular season. If San Antonio can push those numbers down to league average, the margin becomes extremely thin.

Betting Line: Thunder -3 | Over/Under 212 | Series odds: OKC -175, San Antonio +155

title: Ja Morant suspended eight games for conduct detrimental to the league
content: Memphis Grizzlies guard Ja Morant received an eight-game suspension from the NBA for conduct detrimental to the league following an incident at a Memphis nightclub in January.
corpus:
The NBA announced Tuesday that Memphis Grizzlies guard Ja Morant has been suspended eight games without pay for conduct detrimental to the league stemming from an incident at a Memphis entertainment venue in January.

The suspension is effective immediately, meaning Morant will miss the Grizzlies' next eight games. Memphis, currently 32-27 and holding the eighth seed in the Western Conference, faces a difficult stretch with three road games against top-10 teams during his absence.

"We accept the league's decision and support its authority to enforce the standards of conduct," Grizzlies general manager Zach Kleiman said in a statement. "We have had direct conversations with Ja about our expectations and the importance of representing himself, his teammates, and our organization in a way that reflects our values."

In a brief statement released through his agent, Morant said he was disappointed in himself and committed to demonstrating better judgment going forward. He did not specify the nature of the incident.

Morant was averaging 24.8 points and 8.9 assists prior to the suspension — a return to form following his career-year in 2024-25. His absence deals a significant blow to Memphis's playoff positioning in what remains a tightly contested Western Conference.

title: Bucks eliminate Pacers in seven games after Giannis 50-point masterpiece
content: Giannis Antetokounmpo scored 50 points in a Game 7 win to send Milwaukee to the second round for the fourth consecutive year.
corpus:
Giannis Antetokounmpo delivered the signature playoff performance of his career Thursday night, scoring 50 points on 20-of-29 shooting to lead the Milwaukee Bucks past the Indiana Pacers 121-109 in Game 7 at Fiserv Forum.

Antetokounmpo reached the 50-point mark with 4:11 remaining, receiving a standing ovation from the capacity crowd. He finished 8-of-10 from the free-throw line — a marked improvement over his historical playoff struggles — and added 13 rebounds and four assists.

"I told my teammates: this is our time," Antetokounmpo said. "I didn't want it to end. I wanted to go home and get ready for round two."

Haliburton had 34 points and 12 assists for Indiana in a series he carried almost single-handedly, averaging 30.4 and 10.9. But Milwaukee's defensive adjustments — specifically using Khris Middleton to shadow Haliburton off the ball — disrupted Indiana's offensive rhythm at critical moments in the final quarter.

The Bucks will meet the Cavaliers in the second round — a rematch of the 2022 series Milwaukee won in five games. Antetokounmpo now has six 40-point playoff games in his career, second-most in franchise history behind Kareem Abdul-Jabbar's nine.

title: Paolo Banchero named to All-NBA First Team for second consecutive season
content: Orlando Magic forward Paolo Banchero earned his second consecutive All-NBA First Team selection after averaging 27.8 points, 8.4 rebounds, and 6.1 assists in a breakout third professional season.
corpus:
Orlando Magic forward Paolo Banchero received his second consecutive All-NBA First Team selection Wednesday, cementing his status as one of the two or three best forwards in the league at just 23 years old.

Banchero averaged 27.8 points, 8.4 rebounds, and 6.1 assists per game this season, setting career highs in each category. He recorded 14 triple-doubles and became the first Magic player to average 27-8-6 in a season since the franchise's founding in 1989.

"The work he has put in to develop his playmaking is extraordinary," Magic coach Jamahl Mosley said. "He's not just a scorer anymore — he's a quarterback for our offense and one of the most complete players in the East."

Banchero received 89 votes for a first-team spot, compared to 76 for Milwaukee's Giannis Antetokounmpo and 71 for Boston's Jaylen Brown. The remaining first-team spots went to Shai Gilgeous-Alexander and Nikola Jokic.

Despite the individual accolades, Banchero and the Magic were eliminated in the first round by Detroit — a result Banchero called "unacceptable" in his exit interview. He noted that Orlando's seven-game loss without a fully healthy roster still feels like a missed opportunity ahead of what he expects to be a championship-caliber season in 2026-27.

title: Celtics rally from 19 down to beat 76ers 114-108 in Game 7
content: Boston erased a 19-point second-half deficit — the largest Game 7 comeback in franchise history — to advance to the Eastern Conference semifinals with a 114-108 victory over Philadelphia.
corpus:
The Boston Celtics staged the most dramatic comeback in their playoff history Saturday night, erasing a 19-point second-half deficit to defeat the Philadelphia 76ers 114-108 in Game 7 at TD Garden and advance to the Eastern Conference semifinals.

Jaylen Brown led the charge with 36 points, 10 rebounds, and six assists, including 14 points in a decisive 22-4 run that flipped the game's complexion entirely in the third quarter. Jayson Tatum, who played through visible discomfort in his left knee, added 24 points and nine assists.

The Celtics trailed 73-54 midway through the third quarter before mounting the comeback. Boston made nine three-pointers in the third period alone — one short of the NBA playoff record — while Philadelphia went cold from the field, connecting on just 3-of-17 attempts in the frame.

"We were dead and came back to life," Tatum said. "That's the character of this team. Nobody panicked. We just kept executing."

Tyrese Maxey had 32 points for Philadelphia, and Joel Embiid added 28 despite his abdominal injury, but the 76ers could not hold their lead as Boston's defense tightened in the final 18 minutes. The Celtics now advance to meet the Cavaliers in a rematch of the 2023 second round.

title: Nets hire former Warriors assistant as new head coach
content: The Brooklyn Nets have hired Golden State Warriors assistant coach Kenny Natt as their new head coach, ending a seven-week head coaching search following the firing of Jordi Fernandez.
corpus:
The Brooklyn Nets have hired Kenny Natt, who spent the past four seasons as a lead assistant with the Golden State Warriors, as their new head coach, the team announced Monday. The move ends a 52-day coaching search that followed the midseason firing of Jordi Fernandez.

Natt, 61, spent 20 years as an NBA assistant before interviewing for the Nets vacancy. He was the primary architect of Golden State's defensive system over the past three seasons, overseeing a unit that ranked in the top ten in defensive rating despite significant roster turnover.

"Kenny brings a championship culture and a clear identity," Nets general manager Sean Marks said. "He knows how to develop young talent and create accountability. Those were our two non-negotiable criteria for this hire."

Brooklyn will be Natt's first head coaching position after several previous near-misses. He reportedly turned down a head coaching interview with the Portland Trail Blazers in 2024 to remain on Steve Kerr's staff.

The Nets finished 26-56 this season and enter the offseason with three first-round draft picks over the next two years and significant cap space. Natt faces the task of rebuilding a franchise that has not reached the playoffs since 2022 and has cycled through five head coaches in four seasons.

title: Stephen Curry passes Ray Allen to become all-time three-point leader
content: Stephen Curry set the all-time record for three-pointers made in franchise history in Tuesday's win over Portland, becoming the undisputed greatest long-range shooter in NBA history.
corpus:
Stephen Curry etched his name permanently into NBA history Tuesday night in San Francisco, making the three-pointer that pushed him past Ray Allen's previous franchise-level milestone to become the undisputed all-time leader across the league's full three-point records database.

Curry connected from 27 feet in the second quarter, and the Chase Center crowd erupted before the significance of the shot was even announced. A video tribute played on the arena's scoreboard as Curry's teammates rushed the floor to embrace him.

"He's changed how we think about the game," Warriors owner Joe Lacob said from courtside. "What Ray did was extraordinary. What Steph has done is something no one saw coming and no one will replicate."

Curry finished the night with 27 points on 9-of-16 shooting, including the landmark three. He is now 43 ahead of the next active player in career three-pointers made.

Allen, watching via a televised interview from his home in Connecticut, called Curry "the greatest shooter who has ever touched a basketball" and extended his congratulations. The recognition bridged two generations of the game's evolution at the three-point line.

title: Heat's Jimmy Butler faces NBA investigation following conduct report
content: The NBA is investigating Miami Heat forward Jimmy Butler for conduct alleged to be detrimental to teammates and the organization, according to sources with knowledge of the inquiry.
corpus:
The NBA has opened a formal investigation into Miami Heat forward Jimmy Butler following reports of conduct that multiple sources describe as disruptive to the team's locker room and detrimental to organizational operations, ESPN has learned.

The investigation, which began last week, is examining specific incidents from the current season including Butler's interactions with teammates during practices and his reported resistance to coaching staff directives. Sources said the league reached out to Heat management after receiving a complaint filed through official league channels.

Butler's representatives denied that any conduct violated league policy and said Butler has complied fully with the investigation. "Jimmy has been nothing but professional and his record of on-court performance speaks for itself," said his agent.

The Heat declined to comment beyond confirming that the organization is cooperating with the league's inquiry. Coach Erik Spoelstra has continued to start Butler in all games during the investigation period.

Butler is averaging 23.1 points, 6.2 rebounds, and 5.8 assists per game this season. His relationship with the Miami organization has been a topic of speculation throughout the year, and league sources indicated trade interest from multiple teams at the February deadline was significant.

title: Cavaliers-Bucks second-round preview: Mitchell versus Giannis in heavyweight clash
content: Donovan Mitchell and Giannis Antetokounmpo lead their teams into a rematch of the 2022 second-round series as Cleveland hosts Milwaukee in Game 1 of the Eastern Conference semifinals.
corpus:
The Cleveland Cavaliers and Milwaukee Bucks tip off their Eastern Conference second-round series Wednesday at Rocket Mortgage FieldHouse, renewing a rivalry defined by exceptional individual play and defensive chess matches.

The central matchup: Donovan Mitchell versus Giannis Antetokounmpo — two players fresh off historic first-round performances. Mitchell averaged 40.4 points in his Game 6-7 stretch against Toronto; Antetokounmpo averaged 45.5 points in his final two games against Indiana. There may not be a higher-octane individual matchup anywhere in the postseason.

Cleveland's formula is clear: contain Giannis in the paint and force Milwaukee to beat them with outside shooting. The Bucks ranked 19th from three this season at 35.4%. If Jarrett Allen can limit Giannis's damage inside the arc, Cleveland's perimeter defense should manage the rest.

Milwaukee's path runs through breaking Cleveland's half-court defense. The Cavaliers ranked second in defensive rating this season and held opponents to 39.2% overall shooting in the first round against Toronto. Brook Lopez, a capable floor-spacer, gives the Bucks a wrinkle that Indiana lacked.

Betting Line: Cavaliers -1 | Over/Under 216 | Series odds even at -110 each side

title: NBA All-Star Game 2026: West beats East 211-198 in record-setting offensive showcase
content: The Western Conference defeated the East 211-198 in the most offensively productive All-Star Game in NBA history, with Shai Gilgeous-Alexander winning MVP honors with 52 points.
corpus:
The 2026 NBA All-Star Game lived up to its billing as a showcase of the league's offensive firepower, with the Western Conference defeating the Eastern Conference 211-198 in a record-setting contest at State Farm Arena in Atlanta. The combined 409 points surpassed the previous record by 11.

Shai Gilgeous-Alexander was unstoppable throughout, finishing with 52 points on 18-of-26 shooting from the field. He made 8-of-11 three-pointers and added 11 assists in 28 minutes. The performance drew a standing ovation from a crowd that included former stars from four decades of NBA history.

"I had a feeling early that every shot was going in," Gilgeous-Alexander said. "When you're playing with the best players in the world and nobody's really defending, that's the closest to a video game feeling basketball can get."

LeBron James, 41 and the oldest All-Star participant in NBA history, played 18 minutes and scored 14 points in what may have been his final All-Star appearance. The crowd gave him multiple standing ovations throughout the night.

Victor Wembanyama put on a highlight-reel performance with 34 points, six blocks, and three alley-oop dunks. Giannis Antetokounmpo contributed 39 points from the East side. Neither team showed any interest in defense, combining for 81 field goals and 41 three-pointers made.

title: Knicks-Pistons second-round series opens with defensive battle in New York
content: The Knicks held Detroit to 84 points in a 101-84 Game 1 victory to open their Eastern Conference semifinal series, with Jalen Brunson scoring 31 points.
corpus:
The New York Knicks opened their Eastern Conference second-round series against the Detroit Pistons with a dominant 101-84 win at Madison Square Garden on Tuesday, using suffocating half-court defense and Jalen Brunson's 31-point performance to establish home-court control.

Brunson was the story of the night, scoring 19 of his 31 points in the second half as the Pistons attempted to chip away at a 12-point halftime deficit. He converted a series of clutch mid-range jumpers over Detroit's switching defense and drew six second-half fouls.

Detroit's Jalen Duren, whose 22-point Game 7 performance had fueled the Pistons' first-round upset of Orlando, was largely neutralized. Karl-Anthony Towns stayed in front of him on the perimeter and forced Duren into a 7-of-18 shooting night.

"We knew they'd be fired up," Brunson said. "Nobody expected them to be here. Our job was to make it physical from the start and remind them they're playing the Knicks in the Garden."

Mikal Bridges added 19 points and locked down Detroit's Cade Cunningham, limiting him to 14 points on 5-of-17 shooting. The Pistons, who are playing their deepest playoff run since 2008, will need to solve New York's defense in Game 2 on Thursday.

title: LeBron James announces he will return for 23rd NBA season
content: LeBron James confirmed Monday that he will play a 23rd NBA season, dispelling retirement speculation that had intensified following Los Angeles's playoff run.
corpus:
LeBron James put an end to months of retirement speculation Monday, announcing at a postgame media session that he intends to return to the Los Angeles Lakers for his 23rd professional season.

"I've got more in the tank," James, 41, told reporters. "When I feel like I can't contribute at a high level anymore, then we'll have a different conversation. But I'm not there yet. Not even close."

James averaged 21.8 points, 8.1 rebounds, and 7.9 assists this season — numbers that would represent the career peak for the overwhelming majority of NBA players and are extraordinary for a player his age. He played 68 games, the most in a single season since 2018-19.

The announcement likely keeps the Lakers in championship-contention mode for at least one more cycle. General manager Rob Pelinka confirmed the team will attempt to build around James and Anthony Davis with aggressive offseason moves, targeting at least one more All-Star caliber player before training camp.

James also addressed the possibility of playing alongside his son Bronny, who is completing his rookie season. "That's a special thing between a father and son. I don't take it for granted. But I also know we're competing to win a championship — that's always been the standard."

title: NBA Defensive Player of the Year: Wembanyama wins award in second season
content: Victor Wembanyama claimed the 2025-26 Defensive Player of the Year award in his second NBA season, becoming the first player since Dwight Howard to win the award in his sophomore campaign.
corpus:
Victor Wembanyama was named the 2025-26 NBA Defensive Player of the Year on Thursday, claiming the award in his second professional season and becoming the first player since Dwight Howard in 2009 to win the honor that early in a career.

Wembanyama led the league with 4.1 blocks per game and ranked first in defensive win shares among all players. San Antonio's defense improved from 18th in defensive rating his rookie year to 4th this season — the largest single-season defensive improvement by any team in the past decade.

"He makes every defender on the floor better because teams have to account for him on every possession," Spurs coach Gregg Popovich said. "You can't drive. You can't post up. You have to plan around him from the opening tip, and most of the time you still fail."

Wembanyama received 78 of 100 votes, with Boston's Jaylen Brown and Milwaukee's Giannis Antetokounmpo accounting for most of the remaining ballots. He joins Hakeem Olajuwon and David Robinson as the only players to average more than four blocks per game in consecutive seasons.

The award adds to Wembanyama's growing trophy case alongside his 2025 Rookie of the Year award. He finished fifth in MVP voting — a remarkable achievement for a player whose primary reputation remains defensive.

title: Jokic erupts for 51 points in overtime win against Lakers
content: Nikola Jokic posted a 51-point, 18-rebound, 9-assist line in triple overtime as Denver defeated Los Angeles 143-138 to take a 3-1 lead in their Western Conference semifinal series.
corpus:
Nikola Jokic authored arguably the greatest individual playoff performance in Denver Nuggets history Friday night, posting 51 points, 18 rebounds, and 9 assists across 58 extraordinary minutes as the Nuggets outlasted the Los Angeles Lakers 143-138 in triple overtime to take a commanding 3-1 series lead.

Jokic was tireless across three overtime periods, converting an array of post-up baskets, mid-range pull-ups, and running hook shots while playing without a single minute of rest after the third quarter. He scored Denver's final 11 points in regulation — including the tying layup with 4.1 seconds remaining — and added 16 more across the overtime periods.

Anthony Davis matched Jokic as best he could, finishing with 44 points and 16 rebounds in 57 minutes. But Los Angeles could not close the Nuggets out in any of the three extra periods, unraveling at the free-throw line and committing four turnovers in the final overtime session.

"He's the best player in the world tonight, and I've been watching the best player in the world for a long time," LeBron James said. "What he did was special."

Damian Lillard had 29 points but shot 3-of-11 from three-point range, negating much of his spacing value. Denver, which lost Games 1 and 2 at home, has now won three straight and needs one more victory to advance.

title: Celtics-Cavaliers second-round series: Three things to watch
content: Boston and Cleveland open their Eastern Conference semifinal series Friday at TD Garden in a rematch with championship implications for both franchises.
corpus:
The Boston Celtics and Cleveland Cavaliers meet Friday in the Eastern Conference semifinals — a highly anticipated rematch between the East's top seed and the team that pushed them hardest in the 2023 playoffs. Here are three storylines that will shape the series.

1. Tatum's knee.
Jayson Tatum played through obvious discomfort in the second half of the Game 7 win over Philadelphia. He has not practiced fully since. Boston will not reveal the severity, but opponents will probe his left side repeatedly to test his lateral movement and explosion. If Tatum is limited to 70% capacity, Cleveland's defense has a real opening.

2. Mitchell's playoff form.
Donovan Mitchell is playing the best basketball of his career. His 38-point Game 7 effort and series-winning layup against Toronto were the kind of moments that define legacies. He has averaged 37.6 points in his last five playoff games. Boston allowed Philadelphia's Maxey to score 30 twice in the first round — Mitchell is a more dangerous and complete scorer.

3. The big-man matchup.
Jarrett Allen versus Kristaps Porzingis is a fascinating asymmetry. Allen is a better rebounder and rim protector; Porzingis is a far greater floor-spacer. Cleveland's offense runs in the paint — they rank first in paint points. Porzingis's ability to credibly protect the rim without giving up three-point looks will determine how freely Boston can attack.

Betting Line: Celtics -2 | Over/Under 214

title: Victor Wembanyama named All-NBA First Team for the first time
content: San Antonio Spurs center Victor Wembanyama received his first All-NBA First Team selection after posting 26.7 points, 12.1 rebounds, and 4.1 blocks per game in his second professional season.
corpus:
San Antonio Spurs center Victor Wembanyama was named to the All-NBA First Team on Wednesday for the first time in his career, becoming the youngest player to earn the first-team honor since LeBron James in 2006.

Wembanyama averaged 26.7 points, 12.1 rebounds, 4.1 blocks, and 3.9 assists per game while shooting 54.3% from the field. He led the league in blocks for the second consecutive season and ranked second in rebounds among all players.

"The improvement from year one to year two is something I've never seen before," Spurs coach Gregg Popovich said. "His understanding of the game, his conditioning, his post footwork — it's all developed at a rate that isn't supposed to be possible for someone his age."

Wembanyama joined Nikola Jokic, Shai Gilgeous-Alexander, Paolo Banchero, and Jaylen Brown on the first team, displacing LeBron James for the first time since 2007. He was a unanimous selection, receiving all 100 first-team votes.

Off the court, Wembanyama has become one of the league's most prominent figures, with merchandise sales ranking third in the league and his jersey ranking No. 1 internationally. San Antonio's average attendance increased by 14% in his second season.

title: Kevin Durant officially retires after 18 NBA seasons
content: Kevin Durant announced his retirement from professional basketball Thursday, ending an 18-year career that produced two NBA championships, an MVP award, and 14 All-Star selections.
corpus:
Kevin Durant announced his retirement from professional basketball Thursday in a statement released through his foundation, concluding one of the most prolific scoring careers in NBA history. Durant, 37, spent his final season with the Phoenix Suns after declining to exercise a player option last summer.

"Basketball gave me everything," Durant said in the statement. "I got to travel the world, compete with the best players alive, and be part of moments that will live forever. That's all any kid from PG County could ask for."

Durant finishes with 28,743 career points — fourth all-time — along with two NBA championships with Golden State in 2017 and 2018, one MVP award, two Finals MVP awards, and 14 All-Star appearances. He is widely regarded as the most skilled scorer in league history based on his combination of size, footwork, and shooting range.

Former teammates and rivals flooded social media with tributes. LeBron James wrote: "One of one. The most gifted scorer who ever picked up a basketball. I'm grateful I got to share a floor with you." Steph Curry called Durant "a brother and the most unguardable player I've ever seen."

Durant confirmed he will pursue basketball-related opportunities in media and ownership, and has already held preliminary conversations with three NBA franchises about possible ownership stakes.

title: NBA Sixth Man of the Year: Jordan Clarkson wins award for third time
content: Utah Jazz guard Jordan Clarkson claimed his third Sixth Man of the Year award after averaging 21.4 points off the bench — the highest mark by a reserve in over a decade.
corpus:
Utah Jazz guard Jordan Clarkson won the 2025-26 NBA Sixth Man of the Year award for a record third time Thursday, becoming the first player to claim the award three times in league history and setting a new standard for bench scoring with his 21.4 point average.

Clarkson averaged 21.4 points, 4.1 assists, and 3.8 rebounds per game while shooting 47.1% from the field and 40.8% from three. He scored 30 or more points in 12 games as a reserve — the most 30-point bench performances in any season in NBA history.

"I always wanted to prove that coming off the bench is a position of respect, not a demotion," Clarkson said. "Three of these says I've done that. But honestly, the wins matter more."

Clarkson edged Indiana's Obi Toppin and Dallas's Tim Hardaway Jr. in the voting. He received 68 of 100 first-place ballots — a comfortable margin that reflected his unique production level among reserve players.

Utah, a franchise in transition, finished 41-41 and reached the play-in tournament largely because of Clarkson's ability to provide instant offense. Coach Will Hardy said Clarkson is "probably the most efficient scorer-off-the-bench the league has ever seen."

title: NBA Coach of the Year: Monty Williams wins award after Pistons' surprise run
content: Detroit Pistons coach Monty Williams was named the NBA's Coach of the Year after guiding the 38-44 Pistons to a first-round playoff upset and the organization's first second-round appearance since 2008.
corpus:
Monty Williams was named the 2025-26 NBA Coach of the Year on Friday, earning the award after engineering one of the most unlikely team turnarounds in recent memory with the Detroit Pistons, who finished 38-44 in the regular season before eliminating the top-seeded Orlando Magic in the first round.

Williams guided Detroit to a 38-win season despite losing two rotation players to season-ending injuries before the All-Star break. He installed a defensive system that ranked in the league's top ten in the second half of the season and maximized the contributions of Cade Cunningham and Jalen Duren.

"Monty made our guys believe," Pistons general manager Troy Weaver said. "Every single one of them would run through a wall for him. That's not an accident — that's coaching."

Williams received 41 of 100 first-place votes, edging Cleveland's Kenny Atkinson and Oklahoma City's Mark Daigneault. The award marks his second Coach of the Year recognition, following his first in 2023 with Phoenix.

Detroit's playoff success drew significant national attention to a franchise that had been largely invisible for nearly two decades. Williams said his focus during the offseason will be retaining the team's core and adding complementary pieces rather than pursuing a major roster overhaul.

title: Thunder blow out Spurs in Game 1, Gilgeous-Alexander drops 39
content: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander scored 39 points and Oklahoma City dominated San Antonio from wire-to-wire in a 124-99 Game 1 victory to open their Western Conference semifinal series.
corpus:
The Oklahoma City Thunder served notice in their Western Conference semifinal opener Thursday night, dismantling the San Antonio Spurs 124-99 behind Shai Gilgeous-Alexander's 39-point masterclass at Paycom Center.

Gilgeous-Alexander was a step quicker than any Spurs defender all night, scoring from every area of the floor. He hit six three-pointers, converted 11-of-13 free throws, and added six assists and five rebounds in 34 minutes before checking out with the game well in hand in the fourth quarter.

Victor Wembanyama, averaging 32.1 points in the first round, was limited to 21 points on 8-of-21 shooting. OKC's switching defense — designed to keep Wembanyama from getting comfortable in the post — forced him into contested pull-ups and away from his preferred spots.

"He's a handful for anyone. We had a clear plan and we executed it well," Thunder coach Mark Daigneault said. "But this is one game. Victor is too good to be held down for a whole series."

Oklahoma City's depth also asserted itself: Jalen Williams added 22 points, Isaiah Hartenstein controlled the paint with 14 rebounds, and Lu Dort shadowed every Spurs perimeter option without giving an inch. Game 2 is Saturday at Paycom Center.

title: Damian Lillard's playoff debut with Lakers exceeds expectations in series opener
content: Damian Lillard scored 34 points and dished 10 assists in his playoff debut with the Lakers, though Los Angeles fell to Denver 117-114 in overtime in Game 1 of their Western Conference semifinal.
corpus:
Damian Lillard looked every bit the trade-deadline acquisition the Lakers hoped he would be Saturday night at Ball Arena, scoring 34 points and distributing 10 assists in his playoff debut in Los Angeles colors. But it wasn't enough, as the Denver Nuggets escaped with a 117-114 overtime win behind a 41-point effort from Nikola Jokic.

Lillard was sharp all night, making 6-of-12 three-pointers and creating consistently off the pick-and-roll. He drew 10 free-throw attempts and generated 11 points in the fourth quarter to force overtime after the Lakers trailed by eight with 5:40 remaining.

In overtime, however, Lillard's shooting caught up with him. He missed two potential go-ahead three-pointers in the final 90 seconds, and Jokic scored Denver's final seven points — including a step-back jumper that proved to be the game-winner with 14.8 seconds remaining.

Anthony Davis had 28 points and 14 rebounds and was the best defender on the floor for most of the night, holding Jokic to modest production for three quarters before the Nuggets center found another gear in the fourth.

"We played well enough to win," Lakers coach JJ Redick said. "We'll make some adjustments and go after Game 2. I liked almost everything I saw from our group tonight."

title: NBA announces expansion teams in Las Vegas and Seattle for 2028-29 season
content: The NBA officially approved expansion franchises in Las Vegas and Seattle on Tuesday, adding teams 31 and 32 to the league for the 2028-29 season.
corpus:
The NBA Board of Governors voted unanimously Tuesday to approve expansion franchises in Las Vegas and Seattle, officially ending decades of speculation about the league's growth to 32 teams. Both franchises will begin play in the 2028-29 season.

The Las Vegas franchise, backed by a group led by billionaire resort developer Steve Ballmer and including several prominent entertainment industry figures, will play at the newly constructed Allegiant Sports Arena adjacent to the Strip. The franchise group paid a $6.5 billion expansion fee — the largest in sports history.

Seattle's franchise, returning professional basketball to the Pacific Northwest 19 years after the SuperSonics relocated to Oklahoma City, will play at a renovated Climate Pledge Arena under a temporary name until a permanent branding is established. The group paid $6.2 billion.

"This is a historic day for the NBA and for basketball fans across the country," Commissioner Adam Silver said. "Las Vegas has established itself as a world sports capital, and Seattle has proved beyond any doubt that it deserves another franchise."

The expansion draft for both teams will take place in June 2028, with each existing team protecting nine players. Both new franchises will receive lottery picks in the 2028 and 2029 drafts with enhanced odds.

title: Ja Morant's 48-point eruption lifts Grizzlies past Clippers in five-game series
content: Memphis Grizzlies guard Ja Morant poured in 48 points in a clinching Game 5 win over the Los Angeles Clippers, propelling Memphis to the Western Conference second round.
corpus:
Ja Morant was electric Sunday afternoon in Memphis, turning in a 48-point clinic to eliminate the Los Angeles Clippers 119-104 in Game 5 and send the Grizzlies to the second round for the first time since 2022.

Morant was dominant from the opening tip, scoring 18 in the first quarter alone and forcing the Clippers into an early timeout as Crypto.com Arena fell silent. He attacked the rim repeatedly and made seven of the Grizzlies' nine three-pointers en route to his career playoff high.

"This is the version of Ja we've seen in practice all year," Grizzlies coach Taylor Jenkins said. "When he gets in that rhythm there's nothing you can do. We just have to keep getting him the ball."

The Clippers' Kawhi Leonard was held to 23 points on 8-of-21 shooting with a hobbled right knee limiting his burst. Paul George added 17 but was unable to generate sustained runs. Los Angeles's bench scored just 12 combined points.

Desmond Bane contributed 21 points in support as the Grizzlies dominated the glass 48-32 and held the Clippers to 41.3% shooting. Memphis advances to face Oklahoma City or San Antonio in the second round — a matchup that will test Morant against a significantly higher defensive ceiling.

title: Nets' Ben Simmons retires from professional basketball at age 30
content: Ben Simmons officially announced his retirement from professional basketball at age 30, citing chronic back issues that have limited him to 51 games over the past four seasons combined.
corpus:
Ben Simmons officially announced his retirement from professional basketball at age 30 Tuesday, ending a career that promised far more than it ultimately delivered due to persistent back injuries and personal challenges that prevented him from fulfilling his considerable athletic potential.

Simmons was selected No. 1 overall by Philadelphia in 2016 and won Rookie of the Year in 2018. He made three All-Star teams and was named to the All-Defensive First Team twice. However, he played just 51 games across the final four seasons of his career due to a lumbar disc issue that required surgery in 2023 and has recurred multiple times since.

"I gave everything I had to this game and to my teams," Simmons said in a statement released through his agent. "My body just wouldn't let me keep going. I'm at peace with that."

Simmons's career ended with controversy still attached — his refusal to play in the 2021 playoffs with Philadelphia remains one of the most discussed incidents in recent league history. But those close to him say he made peace with that chapter years ago.

He leaves the game averaging 15.9 points, 8.1 rebounds, and 7.7 assists per game across 399 career regular-season appearances. Many scouts still regard him as among the two or three most physically gifted players of his generation.

title: OKC-Spurs semifinal: Wembanyama rallies San Antonio with 44-point Game 2 win
content: Victor Wembanyama responded to Game 1's struggles with a 44-point, 15-rebound performance that lifted San Antonio to a 113-104 win over Oklahoma City to even the series.
corpus:
Victor Wembanyama answered any questions about his resilience Saturday night, delivering a 44-point, 15-rebound, 7-block masterpiece as the San Antonio Spurs defeated the Oklahoma City Thunder 113-104 at AT&T Center to even their Western Conference semifinal series at one game apiece.

After being held to 21 points in Game 1 by Oklahoma City's switching defense, Wembanyama adjusted — posting up more deliberately, operating off back-cuts and lob entries, and seeking contact rather than avoiding it. He drew 14 free throws and converted 13, adding efficiency to a volume performance that was already historic.

"We showed him different looks in Game 1 and he solved them in 48 hours," Thunder coach Mark Daigneault said. "That's what a generational talent does. You adjust, he adjusts better. You have to respect that."

Shai Gilgeous-Alexander had 35 points for Oklahoma City but was forced into a high volume of difficult shots as San Antonio's defense keyed on his early-game rhythm. Jalen Williams added 19 but the Thunder shot 31.4% from three — a number that proved fatal against Wembanyama's interior dominance.

Jeremy Sochan and Keldon Johnson combined for 29 points in supporting roles as the Spurs reclaimed home-court advantage in the series, setting up a critical Game 3 in Oklahoma City on Tuesday.

title: Mitchell, Brown lead Cavaliers, Celtics to wire-to-wire win in Game 1 of East semis
content: Donovan Mitchell and Jaylen Brown each topped 30 points in a physically intense Game 1 that Boston won 112-104 at TD Garden to open the Eastern Conference semifinals.
corpus:
The Eastern Conference semifinals opened Friday with a heavyweight collision that delivered on every expectation. Jaylen Brown led Boston with 34 points as the Celtics defeated the Cleveland Cavaliers 112-104 in a grinding, physical Game 1 at TD Garden.

Brown was dominant in the fourth quarter, scoring 14 of his points in the final frame and repeatedly attacking Cavaliers closeouts in transition. He shot 13-of-24 from the field and 4-of-8 from three. Jayson Tatum, managing his left knee, contributed 22 points and 10 assists but was not at full burst in isolation situations.

Donovan Mitchell answered with 32 points on 11-of-24 shooting, looking every bit the ascending playoff performer who drove Cleveland past Toronto. He made six three-pointers but struggled against Boston's switching, with Jrue Holiday making his life particularly difficult on one-on-one possessions.

"We gave up too many second-chance points and let them score in transition when we didn't need to push," Cavaliers coach Kenny Atkinson said. "But I thought our guys competed at the level this series requires. Game 2 is a must-win."

Boston's Kristaps Porzingis added 17 points and three blocks, successfully limiting Cleveland's paint access throughout. The Celtics have won 12 consecutive home playoff games dating back to last season.

title: Grizzlies' Jaren Jackson Jr. named Defensive Player of the Year finalist
content: Memphis Grizzlies center Jaren Jackson Jr. is among three finalists for the 2025-26 NBA Defensive Player of the Year award, joining Victor Wembanyama and Jaylen Brown on the shortlist.
corpus:
The NBA announced Thursday that Jaren Jackson Jr. of the Memphis Grizzlies is among the three finalists for the 2025-26 Defensive Player of the Year award, alongside San Antonio's Victor Wembanyama and Boston's Jaylen Brown.

Jackson averaged 2.9 blocks per game and ranked third in the league in defensive win shares. Memphis's defense, anchored by Jackson in the paint, held opponents to 45.1% shooting at the rim — the best mark in the league.
