His lateral agility allows him to switch onto guards and wings in a way that traditional centers cannot, giving Memphis defensive flexibility unusual for a team built around a young backcourt.

"JJJ is a chess piece that most teams don't have," Grizzlies coach Taylor Jenkins said. "He gives us the ability to hide matchup problems in ways that change how opponents want to attack."

Jackson won the award in 2022-23 and is widely respected as the premier shot-blocking wing-stopper in the Western Conference behind only Wembanyama. His candidacy this year is complicated by Wembanyama's historically dominant numbers, but Jackson's team-level defensive impact has its own compelling argument.

The winner will be announced at the league's end-of-season awards ceremony next week. Most analysts expect Wembanyama to win, with Jackson and Brown splitting the remaining votes.

title: Lakers eliminate Nuggets in six games to reach Western Conference Finals
content: The Los Angeles Lakers defeated Denver 112-103 in Game 6 to eliminate the Nuggets and advance to the Western Conference Finals for the first time since 2020.
corpus:
LeBron James and the Los Angeles Lakers punched their ticket to the Western Conference Finals Friday night, defeating the Denver Nuggets 112-103 in Game 6 at Ball Arena to end the series four games to two. It is Los Angeles's first conference final appearance in six seasons.

Anthony Davis was the story of the clinching game, posting 35 points and 17 rebounds while defending Nikola Jokic with the physical aggression that had been intermittent in the series' early games. Davis blocked five shots and converted 13-of-20 field-goal attempts.

LeBron James added 26 points, 9 assists, and 8 rebounds in what may have been the finest individual game of a player already confirmed as the greatest in league history. He scored Los Angeles's final nine points in a critical third-quarter run.

Jokic had 38 points and 14 rebounds in a series-defining performance but received insufficient support. Jamal Murray, who had carried Denver at pivotal moments in earlier series, shot 8-of-28 from the field across Games 5 and 6.

The Lakers will face the winner of the Thunder-Spurs series in the Western Conference Finals. Damian Lillard, who averaged 28.1 points across the six-game series, called the advancement "the reason I wanted to come here."

title: Pelicans-Thunder first-round preview: Can Zion's return spark New Orleans?
content: The New Orleans Pelicans enter the first round as a six-seed hoping that a healthy Zion Williamson can challenge the top-seeded Oklahoma City Thunder.
corpus:
The New Orleans Pelicans enter Saturday's first-round opener against the Oklahoma City Thunder as heavy underdogs but with a wildcard that no betting line can fully capture: a healthy Zion Williamson.

Williamson, who returned from his hamstring injury in mid-March and played 23 games before season's end, averaged 28.7 points and 8.3 rebounds in that closing stretch — numbers that suggested he had fully recovered his pre-injury explosiveness. If that version shows up against Oklahoma City, New Orleans can compete with anyone.

But the Thunder present a daunting defensive challenge. Oklahoma City ranked first in defensive rating during the regular season and will deploy Jalen Williams — one of the most versatile defenders in the league — as Williamson's primary check. SGA is also capable of shifting onto Williamson in switch-heavy situations without conceding much.

CJ McCollum gives New Orleans a legitimate playmaking option alongside Williamson, and Brandon Ingram has found his best form late in the season. The Pelicans have been a team built for the regular season in recent years; their playoff ceiling with this group remains genuinely unknown.

Betting Line: Thunder -9.5 | Over/Under 224 | Series odds: OKC -900, New Orleans +600

title: Bucks trade Khris Middleton to Clippers for draft picks and salary relief
content: The Milwaukee Bucks traded veteran forward Khris Middleton to the Los Angeles Clippers at the trade deadline in exchange for two first-round picks and a salary-cap-clearing package.
corpus:
The Milwaukee Bucks traded veteran forward Khris Middleton to the Los Angeles Clippers Friday at the NBA trade deadline in exchange for two unprotected first-round picks and an expiring contract, a league source confirmed to ESPN.

Middleton, 34, struggled with knee soreness early this season and played just 44 games before the deadline. He is in the final year of his contract at $36 million and will become an unrestricted free agent this summer. The trade gives Milwaukee cap flexibility and two assets to use in offseason negotiations.

Clippers president of basketball operations Lawrence Frank believes Middleton's ability to create mid-range looks and shoot off the catch gives Los Angeles a secondary ball-handler alongside Kawhi Leonard. The Clippers entered the deadline 33-24, competing for the fourth seed in the West.

"Khris is a proven playoff performer and a perfect fit for what we're trying to do," Frank said. "He gives us spacing, shot creation, and championship experience in the moments that matter most."

Milwaukee's willingness to move a franchise cornerstone signals the organization's acknowledgment that the Giannis-Middleton era has run its course. The Bucks remain committed to building around Antetokounmpo, but the path forward will involve new supporting pieces rather than attempting to revive this particular partnership.

title: 2026 NBA Finals preview: Lakers vs. Thunder for championship
content: The Los Angeles Lakers and Oklahoma City Thunder meet in the NBA Finals for the first time, bringing together LeBron James and Shai Gilgeous-Alexander in a generational passing-of-the-torch series.
corpus:
The 2026 NBA Finals pit the Los Angeles Lakers against the Oklahoma City Thunder in a series that feels as much like a coronation as a competition — though whose coronation remains the central question.

LeBron James, 41, has carried Los Angeles through two grueling series with performances that have defied every biological expectation. He enters the Finals averaging 27.4 points, 9.1 rebounds, and 9.8 assists across 12 playoff games. If he lifts the Lakers to a championship, there will be no remaining debate about the greatest career in professional basketball history.

But Shai Gilgeous-Alexander, 27, is playing the best basketball of his life. He averaged 33.8 points against a San Antonio defense built specifically to neutralize him and arrived in the Finals as the consensus favorite for Finals MVP regardless of the series outcome. His supporting cast — Williams, Holmgren, and Dort — are all in their prime.

The matchups are fascinating at every position. Davis versus Holmgren is a battle of the most efficient big men in each conference. Lillard versus Williams carries enormous implications for shot creation and defensive accountability. And the coach's chess match between JJ Redick, in his first Finals appearance, and Mark Daigneault, in his second, will be scrutinized on every possession.

Finals Odds: Thunder -130 | Lakers +110 | Over/Under 218.5
